EMS PM Team · May 2026

PM Studio.

Your AI coworker actually works.
May 2026 · For PM, Dev, Test, Design & beyond
The honest question

By now, you've heard about a lot of AI tools.

So — why are we here today to share yet another one?

External
Claude · ChatGPT · Gemini
  • Powerful, general-purpose models
  • Great at generic reasoning and writing
  • No access to internal data or tools
Internal
Copilot · GitHub Copilot · Internal Tools
  • Connected to your data and identity
  • Multiple team-built tools
  • Inside the firewall, broad coverage
Reality Check #1

Generic AI tools hit a ceiling fast.

Three gaps every PM runs into within the first week.

GAP 01
Lack of context
They don't know our org, projects, partners, or history.
GAP 02
Lack of our practice
They don't know how we write specs, run reviews, or ship features.
GAP 03
Lack of internal connections
They can't reach our docs, dashboards, code, or systems.
Takeaway
Great for generic tasks. Not great for customized internal efforts.
Reality Check #2

M365 Copilot has the context — but still falls short.

Useful for internal search. Still not enough to work very well.

01
Context is broad but shallow
M365 Copilot sees a lot — but not clean or deep on any one project.
02
Cross-project hallucination
Content from Project A leaks into Project B. Trust erodes fast.
03
Skill gaps
No specific workflows for how PMs actually work day-to-day.
Takeaway
Useful for internal search. Still not enough to work very well.
Reality Check #3

CLI-based internal tools work — but stay locked behind text.

CLI scripts, GitHub Copilot prompts, VS Code chat — PM Studio v1.0 lived here too. Powerful, but no canvas to think on.

01
No rich canvas for iteration
Conversation in chat, output in files, preview in another window. Nothing lives side-by-side.
02
No side-by-side knowledge views
Can't view a doc, a chart, and a draft together to reason across them in one place.
03
Knowledge scattered across projects
Files and conversations live in separate folders. Context drifts between sessions.
Takeaway
The engine works. What was missing: a knowledge canvas everyone can think on, side-by-side.
What we actually want

The real goal isn't more AI. It's useful AI.

Save you from the 70% plumbing — and supercharge the 30% that matters.
70% Plumbing
30% Judgment
The 70% — Plumbing
  • Switching context across 5+ tools and systems
  • Hunting down the right doc, dashboard, or thread
  • Re-explaining your practice and project every time
  • Formatting, summarizing, status-chasing, note triage
AI takes it.
The 30% — Judgment
  • Frame — defining the right problem
  • Decide — judgment calls and trade-offs
  • Align — strategy and stakeholder alignment
  • Own — review critically; you own the decision
AI supercharges it.

Introducing PM Studio - Built for how PMs actually work.

One Project = One Agent

Each agent ships with three things — no setup, no prompt engineering.

01 / SYSTEM PROMPT
Anchored on you
Profile, discipline, and how you work — built in.
02 / CONTEXT
Scoped to the project
Only what's relevant. No cross-project bleed.
03 / SKILLS
Tailored to you
Daily debrief, spec drafting, prototyping.
